items.title: Pick a hero image — The HSA is the only triple-tax-advantaged account in the code
items.summary: Placeholder hero card written for draft `ll-2026-05-28-001-hsa-contribution` (reason: no-key). Set GEMINI_API_KEY and re-run to get a real image at the same cache path.
items.prompt: The HSA is the only triple-tax-advantaged account in the code. If you have a qualifying high-deductible health plan, money you put in a Health Savings Account is the only kind of money the IRS gives you three breaks on — your contribution comes off taxable income, the balance grows tax-free, and qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free. Plain English: every dollar in does work three times. Style: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text.
items.style_constraint: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text

items.title: Pick a hero image — The Traditional IRA deduction: a quiet line on Schedule 1 that pays for itself
items.summary: Placeholder hero card written for draft `ll-2026-05-28-002-traditional-ira` (reason: no-key). Set GEMINI_API_KEY and re-run to get a real image at the same cache path.
items.prompt: The Traditional IRA deduction: a quiet line on Schedule 1 that pays for itself. A traditional IRA is a personal retirement account whose contribution can be subtracted from your taxable income this year if you qualify. The money then grows tax-deferred until retirement. Whether the deduction is full, partial, or zero depends on your income and whether you (or a spouse) have a workplace plan — and the thresholds move every year. Style: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text.
items.style_constraint: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text

items.title: Pick a hero image — The Roth IRA trade: no break today, every break later
items.summary: Placeholder hero card written for draft `ll-2026-05-28-003-roth-ira` (reason: no-key). Set GEMINI_API_KEY and re-run to get a real image at the same cache path.
items.prompt: The Roth IRA trade: no break today, every break later. A Roth IRA does not give you a deduction this year. What it gives you is a future where the entire account — contributions plus decades of growth — comes out tax-free at retirement, provided you meet the qualified-withdrawal rules. It is especially valuable if you expect to be in the same or a higher tax bracket later, and contributions can be pulled back without penalty. Style: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text.
items.style_constraint: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text

items.title: Pick a hero image — Workplace 401(k): the only loophole your employer pays you to use
items.summary: Placeholder hero card written for draft `ll-2026-05-28-004-employer-401k` (reason: no-key). Set GEMINI_API_KEY and re-run to get a real image at the same cache path.
items.prompt: Workplace 401(k): the only loophole your employer pays you to use. A 401(k) (or 403(b) for nonprofit and public employees) lets you direct part of your paycheck into a retirement account before income tax is calculated, lowering your taxable wages for the year. Many employers also match a portion of what you contribute — that match is essentially free compensation that disappears if you do not enroll. Style: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text.
items.style_constraint: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text

items.title: Pick a hero image — The Child Tax Credit, decoded: who qualifies and what's refundable
items.summary: Placeholder hero card written for draft `ll-2026-05-28-005-child-tax-credit` (reason: no-key). Set GEMINI_API_KEY and re-run to get a real image at the same cache path.
items.prompt: The Child Tax Credit, decoded: who qualifies and what's refundable. The Child Tax Credit reduces your tax bill for each qualifying child under the age threshold, and a portion may be refundable through the Additional Child Tax Credit — meaning families can receive value even if it exceeds the tax they owe. The per-child amount, age limit, and phase-out thresholds are set by law and have changed several times, so the rule for the year matters. Style: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text.
items.style_constraint: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text

items.title: Pick a hero image — Two education credits, one tuition bill: how to pick AOTC vs Lifetime Learning
items.summary: Placeholder hero card written for draft `ll-2026-05-28-006-education-credits` (reason: no-key). Set GEMINI_API_KEY and re-run to get a real image at the same cache path.
items.prompt: Two education credits, one tuition bill: how to pick AOTC vs Lifetime Learning. Two credits reward spending on higher education. The American Opportunity Tax Credit applies to the first several years of undergraduate study, is partly refundable, and is generally the more generous of the two. The Lifetime Learning Credit is broader — undergraduate, graduate, and job-skill courses with no year limit — but is non-refundable and smaller. You cannot claim both for the same student in the same year. Style: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text.
items.style_constraint: editorial illustration, clean, brand-tealwarm palette, no text
